payload.report_md:
US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Wednesday 7/22
=========================================================

**[Wednesday 7/22 session outlook] Bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +27 · confidence 43%**

🔮 **Next-session forecast:** The next U.S. regular session is Wednesday, July 22, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST). Data as of 2026-07-21 22:13:30 ET; data quality: fresh.

Executive Summary
------------------
With U.S. markets closed, this brief looks ahead to Wednesday's session. The composite market-state score stands at +27 out of 100, placing conditions in a "Moderate / selective risk-on" regime heading into the open, though confidence in that read is modest at 43%, built on four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured this cycle — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning — which have been reweighted out of the composite and should be kept in mind as a limiting factor on conviction.

Market Diagnosis
-----------------
Heading into Wednesday's open, the major indices closed the prior session firmly higher: the S&P 500 finished at 7,509.20, up 0.89% on the day; the Nasdaq-100 led with a 1.93% gain to 29,155.18; the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.74% to 52,224.64; and the Russell 2000 advanced 1.53% to 2,987.40. That broad-based strength across large-cap, tech-heavy, blue-chip, and small-cap benchmarks alike underpins the constructive setup for the next session.

Beneath the index-level moves, the driver mix is mostly supportive of continued risk appetite. Volatility is acting as a tailwind, the prevailing index trend remains supportive, and the yield curve is likewise constructive. The lone headwind comes from the U.S. dollar, which is working against the risk-on thesis even as the other drivers line up favorably.

On breadth and concentration, the same +27/100 composite score anchors the "Moderate / selective risk-on" characterization, with the 43% confidence level and four-component measurement basis serving as an important caveat — the read should be treated as directional rather than definitive given how much (Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, Positioning) currently sits outside the model.

Sector positioning shows a rotation favoring Energy, Health Care, and Financials as the current leadership group, while Consumer Discretionary, Materials, and Technology lag. Notably, Technology's lagging placement in the sector rotation sits alongside the Nasdaq-100's outsized index-level gain, a nuance worth watching as the new session unfolds.

In rates, the dollar, and commodities, the 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.60%. The Dollar Index (DXY) is at 101.19, up 0.20% on the day — consistent with its role as the one headwind driver noted above. Commodities were broadly firm: WTI crude rose 1.63% to $84.59, and gold climbed 1.83% to $4,083.60.

Outlook for the Wednesday 7/22 Session
----------------------------------------
**Short term.** Going into Wednesday's open (9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST), the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 43% confidence level. This lean would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term view carries the same constructive bias tied to the "Moderate / selective risk-on" read (43% confidence), with the same conditional caveat: a move of the overall score below zero would invalidate this stance.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term outlook likewise leans constructive under the current "Moderate / selective risk-on" classification (43% confidence), again subject to reversal if the composite score turns negative.

Top News
--------
Corporate headlines heading into the new session are dominated by the AI-hardware supply chain: Dell shares jumped on rival Supermicro's strong margin guidance (Investing.com); Supermicro (SMCI) itself soared late on booming margins and a $60 billion order backlog that overshadowed softer revenue, with Dell and HP Enterprise also rallying in sympathy (Investor's Business Daily; Money Morning). Jim Cramer flagged Dell as the next winner following SMCI's preliminary results (Benzinga). Elsewhere, Nvidia's purchase of a 9.3% stake in an AI cloud name sent Nebius (NBIS) sharply higher (Money Morning), and attention turns to Tesla's earnings tonight, with a delivery beat being weighed against Wells Fargo's $130 price target (Money Morning).

- No prior brief — first publication.
